chapter_title.ar: باب الشَّرْطِ فِي النِّكَاحِ وَ مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْهُ وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 65 – The stipulation regarding the marriage, and what is allowed from it, and what is not allowed

text: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنِ ابْنِ بُكَيْرٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي امْرَأَةٍ نَكَحَهَا رَجُلٌ فَأَصْدَقَتْهُ الْمَرْأَةُ وَ شَرَطَتْ عَلَيْهِ أَنَّ بِيَدِهَا الْجِمَاعَ وَ الطَّلَاقَ فَقَالَ خَالَفَ السُّنَّةَ وَ وَلَّى الْحَقَّ مَنْ لَيْسَ أَهْلَهُ وَ قَضَى أَنَّ عَلَى الرَّجُلِ الصَّدَاقَ وَ أَنَّ بِيَدِهِ الْجِمَاعَ وَ الطَّلَاقَ وَ تِلْكَ السُّنَّةُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, form Ibn Bukeyr, from one of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a woman who got married to a man, so the woman kept a dower and stipulated upon him that the copulation and the divorce would be in her hands’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He would be opposing the Sunnah and he has placed the right upon the one who is not rightful for it, and the judgement is that upon the man is the dower, and that in his hand would be the copulation and the divorce, and that is the Sunnah’.
